title: Cacalia chamaepeuces
intro: Cacalia chamaepeuces, also known as Eastern Wild Cress, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cacalia chamaepeuces include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Cacalia chamaepeuces is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to treat conditions such as respiratory infections, digestive issues, and skin disorders.
